market_briefing.summary: Global markets continue to be significantly impacted by the ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict, resulting in high volatility, particularly in energy and precious metals. Recent de-escalatory rhetoric has provided some cooling, but uncertainty remains high.
highlights: The U.S.-Iran conflict continues to dominate global headlines; despite some de-escalatory signals, oil remains highly volatile.
Brent crude oil eased to $107-$108 per barrel, and WTI crude fell to approximately $94 per barrel, down about 1.5% from the previous session.
Gold rebounded today, gaining 1.5% to trade above $4,700 per ounce, recovering from recent downward pressure despite overall monthly declines.
Central banks globally maintain a hawkish stance amid inflationary pressures, with markets pushing Fed rate cut expectations toward 2027.
Asian markets show mixed performance, while European futures saw modest gains due to the slight retreat in energy prices.
